sourceMovie.title: Passengers
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Passengers (2016) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ex Machina
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Passengers clicked for you, Ex Machina (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: 10 Cloverfield Lane
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Passengers clicked for you, 10 Cloverfield Lane (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Passengers?
faq.answer: Try Ex Machina, 10 Cloverfield Lane, Moon—each shares DNA with Passengers while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Passengers?
faq.answer: Passengers (2016) is best described as Drama, Romance, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Passengers worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
